title: Manual H&E Staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7ebg7","text":"The hematoxylin and eosin (H\u0026E) stain is the standard used for microscopic examination of fixed tissue specimens. This procedure establishes a consistent process for the manual preparation of H\u0026E-stained slides from FFPE tissue specimens. H\u0026E staining is compatible with, and frequently performed after tissue-based cyclic immunofluorescence (t-CyCIF), enabling conventional histopathology review to be combined with fluorescence imaging.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"anuoe","text":"This protocol can be performed on the same tissue specimen that has previously undergone t-CyCIF. A protocol for t-CyCIF can be found here: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bjiukkew.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":140,"length":41}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":140,"length":41}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"0F4CE51372EE11EBBE920A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bjiukkew"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"b3qlq","text":"Required Materials","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dshsd","text":"Hematoxylin Solution, Gill No. 3 (Millipore Sigma, GHS332)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4tt1","text":"Eosin Y Solution, 1% aqueous (Millipore Sigma, HT110232)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bsbp1","text":"Coverslips, rectangular 50x24 mm (VWR, 48393-081)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1b6eo","text":"Permount Mounting Medium (Fisher Chemical, SP15-100)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5e0m0","text":"Staining jar","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"49loc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f5rp7","text":"You may also need: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4no87","text":"Forceps","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b0oi8","text":"20X Phosphate Buffered Saline (Thermo Fisher Scientific, 28348)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: After the final imaging step of CyCIF (or IF), soak slides in 1X PBS for 00:30:00 Room temperature and gently detach coverslips from slides using slide forceps.
Remove slides and place on the stain box. Add between 200 µL and 400 µL Hematoxylin solution and let incubate for 00:01:00 Room temperature .
Wash slides by placing them into the staining jar with running tap water for 00:00:30 Room temperature .
Put washed slides back to the staining box. Add between 200 µL and 400 µL Eosin Y solution and incubate for 00:01:00 Room temperature .
Wash slides by placing into the staining jar with running tap water for at least 00:00:30 Room temperature until all the excess red Eosin color is gone.
Air dry slides completely (at least 01:00:00 ), mount slides with 80 µL Permount solution, and gently place coverslips. Slides can be imaged after Overnight .
Wash slides 1-2 times in the staining jar with tap water Room temperature .
creator: Madison Tyler
